action.reasoning: Observation: The current best test balanced_accuracy is 0.8539 from iteration 16 (ensemble_fine.pt: 95% ConvNeXt seed42 + 5% ConvNeXt seed123, tau=1.70). Iteration 19's 3-way ensemble with ResNet50 did not improve (also 0.8539), confirming that the third model adds no value. Analysis: Since training new models has not beaten the existing strong ConvNeXt base, the most efficient way to push the score higher is stronger test-time augmentation (TTA). Currently predict.py only averages original + horizontal-flip logits (2 views). Multi-scale TTA (e.g., 224/240/256 + flips = 6 views) is a standard, zero-training-cost technique that often improves balanced accuracy by smoothing predictions. To maximize benefit, I will re-tune the ensemble weight and logit-adjustment temperature on the validation set using the exact same multi-scale TTA pipeline that will be used at test time. This ensures the hyperparameters are optimal for the true inference setup. Decision: Write a new tuning script (tune_ensemble_tta.py) and update predict.py to support 6-view multi-scale TTA. Run tuning on best_iter9.pt + convnext_s2.pt, save the new ensemble checkpoint, perform a local smoke test, and submit eval.
action.command: python tune_ensemble_tta.py && python predict.py --data_path fake_test --checkpoint checkpoints/ensemble_tta.pt --output fake_out.json && python -c "import json; d=json.load(open('fake_out.json')); print('Smoke test OK, preds count:', len(d['predictions']))"
